channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Witcher 3 Songs of the Past Expansion Will Be Showcased at Gamescom 2026
item.content:encoded:
The developers have announced that players will be receiving a first look at Witcher 3’s Songs of the Past expansion at Gamescom 2026.

The Witcher 3 will be receiving a brand new expansion after being in the market for more than 11 years, with Songs of the Past releasing for the game in 2027. It is speculated that the upcoming expansion will set up the story for the next installment, The Witcher 4; however, players will have to wait until the Gamescom 2026 showcase to know for sure.

A new post on The Witcher’s official X account (formerly Twitter) reveals that players will be seeing that Songs of the Past expansion at Gamecom 2026. The developers have invited players to join them in the entertainment area, where they will showcase the upcoming expansion. It is currently unclear whether CD Projekt Red will present just a trailer or provide a playable demo. Players will have to wait until August 2026 for more information, or another announcement may come soon.

Witcher 3 Devs Insist Songs of the Past is an Expansion and Not A DLC

According to statements from the developers, the upcoming expansion appears to be significant. Marcin Łukaszewski, the social media manager at CD Projekt Red (CDPR), has emphasized that “Songs of the Past” should not be referred to as a DLC, but rather as an expansion. When asked to clarify the distinction, Łukaszewski explained that CDPR views DLCs as minor content additions, typically provided for free, such as extra outfits. In contrast, they consider expansions to be “major pieces of content providing lots of hours of gameplay, including new story, characters, etc.”

In other news, the latest announcement by CD Projekt Red reveals that The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt has sold 65 million copies. Additionally, an updated set of minimum PC system requirements has been provided for the upcoming expansion. What are your thoughts on The Witcher 3’s Songs of the Past expansion being showcased at Gamescom 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Warzone ending support for the PS4 and Xbox One in 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt Hits 65 Million Copies Sold
item.content:encoded:
CD Projekt Red’s earnings presentation reveals that The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt has now sold 65 million copies.

11 years after release, The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt has become a major topic of conversation in the gaming space, all thanks to the new expansion announcements. The Songs of the Past expansion for the title is all set to release in 2027, and it seems it will help drive even more sales for CD Projekt Red’s highly revered series.

New Sales Milestone Revealed for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt

According to CD Projekt Group’s latest earnings, The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt has hit a new sales milestone, selling 65 million copies. The title has cemented itself in the list of the top 10 best-selling games of all time. Last year, the game had sold 60 million copies and has now jumped by 5 million in the span of a year. The number will seemingly keep increasing as time goes on, especially once the third expansion, Songs of the Past, arrives in 2027.

The earnings report also reveals that CD Projekt Group generated 191 PLN in the first quarter of 2026, a 6% year-on-year increase. A statement by the CFO reveals that there was a solid revenue stream in the first 3 months of the year, most of which came from Cyberpunk 2077 and The Witcher 3, along with their addition to Xbox Game Pass Premium and Ultimate. The dev team is also increasing, adding 42 more developers across various projects between February and April 2026.

In other news, The Witcher 3 will get a new set of minimum PC system requirements due to the Songs of the Past expansion. Additionally, an analyst has predicted that CD Projekt Red could spend close to $800 million on The Witcher 4. What are your thoughts on The Witcher 3 selling 65 million copies? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Warzone ending support for the PS4 and Xbox One in 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
